title: Rejection Signal (turn-pair gap)
description: One classified rejection mined from a (assistant_text, user_next_turn) pair. Written by `trinity-local lens-build` (Stage 0 turn-pair gap extraction). Lives one-per-line in ~/.trinity/me/rejections.jsonl. The empirical proof the user rejected a specific response shape — load-bearing for the eval-set builder (#122) and the inversion-test pipeline (#113).

id.description: Stable id (typically `r_NNN` sequential within a lens-build run).

type.description: The four implicit-rejection signal types from the taste-terminal schema. REFRAME = user substitutes a different frame entirely. COMPRESSION = user wanted shorter (validator: user_text wc ≤ model_text/10). REDIRECT = user wanted a structurally different output (validator: model_text was multi-part). SHARPENING = user wanted more precision on the same topic (validator: user_text shares ≥2 keywords with model_text).

model_quote.description: The assistant utterance that triggered the rejection — quoted verbatim so the eval scorer can compare a candidate's response against the same anti-pattern.

user_substitute.description: What the user typed next — their implicit correction. NOT a request to redo; the next turn's content directly substitutes for what the user actually wanted.

why_signal.description: Chairman-extracted rationale for why this counts as a rejection of the named type. Stored so the eval scorer has rubric context.

prompt_id.description: PromptNode id of the user's turn that elicited the rejected response. Null if the index has churned and the prompt can't be re-located.

basin.description: Topic-cluster id (from topics.json). Enables per-basin × rejection_type aggregation.

next_user_turn.description: REFRAME-only — the user's turn AFTER the substitute, used to verify the frame persisted (i.e. wasn't a one-off correction). Empty when not applicable.
